src: διηγήσομαι δὲ τὰ ῥήματα τῆς γραφῆς,
lit: And I shall recount the words of the scripture,
sm: And now I will recount the words of the scripture,

syn.mc: Main clause.
syn.sub: The object-content of the recounting is developed in lines 72-74.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ἐγώ. Verb: διηγήσομαι. Object: τὰ ῥήματα τῆς γραφῆς.
syn.rhet: Programmatic transition from citation to detailed narration.

src: πῶς ὃ θεὸς ἐντέταλται Πωυσεῖ ἐν Αἰγύπτῳ,
ana: πῶς ὁ θεὸς ἐντέταλται Μωυσεῖ ἐν Αἰγύπτῳ,
lit: How God has commanded Moses in Egypt,
sm: how God gave command to Moses in Egypt,
emd: Followed change_log.txt for Πωυσεῖ -> Μωυσεῖ. The raw ὃ before θεὸς is also analyzed as ὁ, since the article is required syntactically here.

syn.mc: Indirect explanatory clause dependent on διηγήσομαι in line 71.
syn.sub: The content clause is introduced by πῶς; within it, a temporal clause follows in lines 73-74.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ θεός. Verb: ἐντέταλται. Indirect object: Μωυσεῖ. Prepositional phrase: ἐν Αἰγύπτῳ.
syn.rhet: Resumes the earlier πῶς-pattern of exposition in a narrative register.

src: ὁπότε βούλεται τὸν μὲν Φαραὼ δῆσαι ὑπὸ μάστιγα,
lit: When he wishes on the one hand to bind Pharaoh under a plague,
sm: when he wishes, on the one hand, to bind Pharaoh under a plague,

syn.mc: Temporal subordinate clause within the content clause of line 72.
syn.sub: ὁπότε introduces a temporal clause; βούλεται governs the infinitives δῆσαι and λῦσαι (line 74).
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὁ θεός. Verb: βούλεται. Infinitival object: δῆσαι with accusative subject/object τὸν Φαραώ; prepositional phrase: ὑπὸ μάστιγα.
syn.rhet: μὲν sets up the balanced infinitival pair completed by line 74.

src: τὸν δὲ ᾿Ισραὴλ λῦσαι ἀπὸ μάστιγος διὰ χειρὸς Μωυσέως.
lit: But to free Israel from plague through the hand of Moses.
sm: but to free Israel from the plague by the hand of Moses.

syn.mc: Completion of the infinitival pair begun in line 73.
syn.sub: Still inside the temporal clause introduced by ὁπότε.
syn.svo: Second infinitival complement to βούλεται: λῦσαι. Object: τὸν Ἰσραήλ. Prepositional phrases: ἀπὸ μάστιγος, διὰ χειρὸς Μωυσέως.
syn.rhet: Balanced antithesis: Pharaoh bound / Israel freed.

src: Ἰδοὺ γάρ, φησίν, λήμψῃ ἄσπιλον ἀμνὸν καὶ ἄμωμον,
lit: For behold, he says, you will take a spotless and blameless lamb,
sm: For, behold, he says, you shall take a spotless and blameless lamb,

syn.mc: Quoted main clause introducing divine instruction.
syn.sub: φησίν is parenthetical; the quotation continues through line 89.
syn.svo: Within the quote, implied subject: σύ. Verb: λήμψῃ. Object: ἄσπιλον ἀμνὸν καὶ ἄμωμον.
syn.rhet: Formal scriptural citation opens with deictic solemnity.

src: καὶ πρὸς ἑσπέραν σφάξεις αὐτὸν μετὰ τῶν υἱῶν ᾿Ισραήλ,
lit: And toward evening you will slaughter it with the sons of Israel,
sm: and toward evening you shall slaughter it with the sons of Israel,

syn.mc: Coordinate clause within the quotation.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Implied subject: σύ. Verb: σφάξεις. Object: αὐτόν. Prepositional phrases: πρὸς ἑσπέραν, μετὰ τῶν υἱῶν Ἰσραήλ.
syn.rhet: Sequential ritual instruction continues in parataxis.

src: καὶ νύκτωρ ἔδεσθε αὐτὸ μετὰ σπουδῆς"
ana: καὶ νύκτωρ ἔδεσθε αὐτὸ μετὰ σπουδῆς·
lit: And by night you will eat it with haste.
sm: And by night you shall eat it in haste.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Coordinate clause within the quotation.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Verb: ἔδεσθε. Object: αὐτό. Adverbial phrases: νύκτωρ, μετὰ σπουδῆς.
syn.rhet: Movement of the ritual continues from taking to slaughtering to eating.

src: ὀστοῦν οὐ συντρίψεις αὐτοῦ
lit: You will not break a bone of it
sm: You shall not break a bone of it

syn.mc: Negative clause within the quotation.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: συντρίψεις. Object: ὀστοῦν. Dependent genitive: αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: A brief apodictic prohibition interrupts the sequence of positive commands.

src: οὕτως, φησίν, ποιήσεις.
lit: Thus, he says, you will do.
sm: Thus, he says, you shall do.

syn.mc: Main clause closing the instruction unit.
syn.sub: φησίν is parenthetical and quotative.
syn.svo: Implied subject: σύ. Verb: ποιήσεις. Adverb: οὕτως.
syn.rhet: Formulaic closure before the next specification.

src: ἐν μιᾷ νυκτὶ ἔδεσθε αὐτὸ" κατὰ πατριὰς καὶ δήμους,
ana: ἐν μιᾷ νυκτὶ ἔδεσθε αὐτὸ κατὰ πατριὰς καὶ δήμους,
lit: In one night you will eat it according to clans and communities,
sm: In a single night you shall eat it by clans and households,
emd: Stray OCR quotation mark removed from within the clause.

syn.mc: Main clause of further instruction.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ὑμεῖς. Verb: ἔδεσθε. Object: αὐτό. Prepositional phrases: ἐν μιᾷ νυκτί, κατὰ πατριὰς καὶ δήμους.
syn.rhet: Returns to distributive ritual detail.

src: περιεζωσμένοι τὰς ὀσφύας ὑμῶν
tok: pf mp ptcp nom pl m
lit: Having girded your loins
sm: with your loins girded,

syn.mc: Participial modifier of the implied subject in line 80.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle gives attendant circumstance or manner.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ὑμεῖς. Participle: περιεζωσμένοι. Object: τὰς ὀσφύας. Possessive genitive: ὑμῶν.
syn.ptc: περιεζωσμένοι expresses the required state in which the meal is to be eaten.
syn.rhet: Graphic ritual posture intensifies urgency.

src: καὶ αἱ ῥάβδοι ἐν ταῖς χερσὶν ὑμῶν.
lit: And the staffs in your hands.
sm: and your staffs in your hands.

syn.mc: Elliptical nominal clause continuing the ritual description.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; an implied verb such as ἔστωσαν or an understood circumstantial relation continues from line 81.
syn.svo: Subject: αἱ ῥάβδοι. Implied locative/circumstantial predication: ἐν ταῖς χερσὶν ὑμῶν.
syn.rhet: The terse nominal style heightens the ready-for-departure posture.

src: ἔστιν γὰρ τοῦτο πάσχα κυρίου,
lit: For this is Passover of the Lord,
sm: For this is the Passover of the Lord,

syn.mc: Main identificatory clause within the quotation.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: τοῦτο. Verb: ἔστιν. Predicate nominative: πάσχα κυρίου.
syn.rhet: Explanatory γάρ gives the theological name of the rite.

src: μνημόσυνον αἰώνιον τοῖς υἱοῖς ἰσραήλ.
lit: An eternal memorial for the sons of Israel.
sm: an everlasting memorial for the sons of Israel.

syn.mc: Appositional/predicative continuation of line 84.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Nominative phrase μνημόσυνον αἰώνιον stands in apposition or further predication to τοῦτο/πάσχα. Dative phrase: τοῖς υἱοῖς Ἰσραήλ.
syn.app: μνημόσυνον αἰώνιον apposes and characterizes πάσχα κυρίου.
syn.rhet: The rite is defined not only by name but by enduring communal function.

src: λαβόντες δὲ τὸ τοῦ προβάτου αἷμα
tok: aor act ptcp nom pl m
lit: And having taken the blood of the sheep
sm: And after taking the blood of the sheep,

syn.mc: Participial phrase introducing the next command.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle modifies the implied addressees of the imperative in line 87.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ὑμεῖς. Participle: λαβόντες. Object: τὸ τοῦ προβάτου αἷμα.
syn.ptc: λαβόντες is attendant circumstance, preparatory to χρίσατε.
syn.rhet: The ritual sequence advances from identification to application.

src: χρίσατε τὰ πρόθυρα τῶν οἰκιῶν ὑμῶν,
lit: Smear the doorposts of your houses,
sm: smear the doorposts of your houses,

syn.mc: Imperative main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; participial modifiers surround the imperative in lines 86 and 88-89.
syn.svo: Verb: χρίσατε. Object: τὰ πρόθυρα. Dependent genitive: τῶν οἰκιῶν ὑμῶν.
syn.rhet: Direct imperative at the center of the ritual instruction.

src: τιθέντες ἐπὶ τοὺς σταθμοὺς τῆς εἰσόδου
tok: pres act ptcp nom pl m
lit: Placing upon the posts of the entrance
sm: placing upon the entrance posts

syn.mc: Participial phrase dependent on the imperative χρίσατε.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ὑμεῖς. Participle: τιθέντες. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τοὺς σταθμοὺς τῆς εἰσόδου.
syn.ptc: τιθέντες gives the manner and extension of the smearing command.
syn.rhet: Keeps the command flowing in participial syntax rather than restarting it.

src: τὸ σημεῖον τοῦ αἵματος" εἰς δυσωπίαν τοῦ ἀγγέλου.
ana: τὸ σημεῖον τοῦ αἵματος εἰς δυσωπίαν τοῦ ἀγγέλου.
lit: The sign of the blood for the appeasing of the angel.
sm: the sign of the blood, for the appeasing of the angel.
emd: Stray OCR quotation mark removed from the noun phrase.

syn.mc: Object phrase completing the participial construction of line 88.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the phrase remains dependent on τιθέντες / χρίσατε.
syn.svo: Object of τιθέντες: τὸ σημεῖον τοῦ αἵματος. Purpose phrase: εἰς δυσωπίαν τοῦ ἀγγέλου.
syn.rhet: The blood-sign is marked as both visible token and apotropaic means.

src: ἰδοὺ γάρ, πατάσσω ἀἰγυπτον
lit: For behold, I strike Egypt
sm: For behold, I am striking Egypt,

syn.mc: Quoted divine main clause.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 91 continues its consequence.
syn.svo: Subject: implied ἐγώ. Verb: πατάσσω. Object: Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: Vivid present intensifies immediacy of judgment.

src: καὶ ἐν μιᾷ νυκτὶ ἀτεκνωθήσεται ἀπὸ κτήνους ἕως ἀνθρώπου.
lit: And in one night it will be made childless from beast as far as man.
sm: and in a single night it will be made bereft, from beast to man.

syn.mc: Coordinate clause continuing the divine declaration of line 90.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied Αἴγυπτος. Verb: ἀτεκνωθήσεται. Temporal phrase: ἐν μιᾷ νυκτί. Range phrase: ἀπὸ κτήνους ἕως ἀνθρώπου.
syn.rhet: The judgment is totalizing, spanning beast and human alike.

src: τότε Μωυσῆς σφάξας τὸ πρόβατον
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: Then Moses, having slaughtered the sheep,
sm: Then Moses, after slaughtering the sheep,

syn.mc: Main narrative frame with participial lead-in.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participles of lines 92-93 lead to the finite verb in line 94.
syn.svo: Subject: Μωυσῆς. Participle: σφάξας. Object: τὸ πρόβατον.
syn.ptc: σφάξας is antecedent action, preparing for the sealing act.
syn.rhet: Narrative execution now matches the preceding divine command.

src: [καὶ] νύκτωρ διατελέσας τὸ μυστήριον μετὰ τῶν υἱῶν σραὴλ
ana: [καὶ] νύκτωρ διατελέσας τὸ μυστήριον μετὰ τῶν υἱῶν Ἰσραὴλ
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: [And] by night having performed the mystery with the sons of Israel
sm: and by night, after performing the mystery with the sons of Israel,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: σραὴλ -> Ἰσραὴλ. The accent of μυστήριον is normalized silently for lexical parsing only.

syn.mc: Second participial phrase continuing line 92.
syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; this phrase still depends on the finite verb in line 94.
syn.svo: Subject: Μωυσῆς. Participle: διατελέσας. Object: τὸ μυστήριον. Accompaniment phrase: μετὰ τῶν υἱῶν Ἰσραήλ.
syn.ptc: διατελέσας adds a second antecedent action before ἐσφράγισεν.
syn.rhet: Participial accumulation mirrors careful ritual completion.

src: ἐσφράγισεν τὰς τῶν οἰκιῶν θύρας
lit: He sealed the doors of the houses
sm: he sealed the doors of the houses

syn.mc: Main clause completing the participial setup of lines 92-93.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Subject: Μωυσῆς. Verb: ἐσφράγισεν. Object: τὰς ... θύρας. Dependent genitive: τῶν οἰκιῶν.
syn.rhet: The ritual action is compressed into a decisive aorist.

src: εἰς φρουρὰν τοῦ λαοῦ καὶ εἰς δυσωπίαν τοῦ ἀγγέλου.
lit: For protection of the people and for appeasing of the angel.
sm: for the protection of the people and for the appeasing of the angel.

syn.mc: Prepositional purpose/result phrase dependent on ἐσφράγισεν.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause.
syn.svo: Purpose phrases: εἰς φρουρὰν τοῦ λαοῦ and εἰς δυσωπίαν τοῦ ἀγγέλου modify the sealing action.
syn.rhet: Balanced double purpose: safeguarding Israel, appeasing the angel.

src: “Οπότε δὲ τὸ πρόβατον σφάζεται
lit: And when the sheep is slaughtered
sm: And when the sheep is slaughtered,

syn.mc: Temporal subordinate clause beginning a long protasis.
syn.sub: Ὁπότε introduces a temporal clause that continues through line 101 and leads to the main clause at line 102.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ πρόβατον. Verb: σφάζεται.
syn.rhet: A new anaphoric temporal sequence begins, mirroring the unfolding rite.

src: καὶ τὸ πάσχα βιβρώσκεται
lit: And the Passover is eaten
sm: and the Passover is eaten,

syn.mc: Coordinate member within the temporal protasis begun in line 97.
syn.sub: Still inside the ὁπότε-clause.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ πάσχα. Verb: βιβρώσκεται.
syn.rhet: Second liturgical action in the rhythmic temporal buildup.

src: καὶ τὸ μυστήριον τελεῖται
lit: And the mystery is accomplished
sm: and the mystery is performed,

syn.mc: Coordinate member within the same temporal protasis.
syn.sub: Still governed by ὁπότε.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ μυστήριον. Verb: τελεῖται.
syn.rhet: Third member deepens the sacrificial and ritual progression.

src: καὶ ὁ λαὸς εὐφραίνεται
lit: And the people rejoices
sm: and the people rejoice,

syn.mc: Coordinate member within the temporal protasis begun in line 97.
syn.sub: The temporal protasis continues beyond this line through line 101 before yielding the main clause at line 102.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ λαός. Verb: εὐφραίνεται.
syn.rhet: The series moves from ritual acts to communal response.
